This stage is cross-cutting presentation infrastructure for “live” terminal feedback: it sits underneath interactive TUI screens and CLI diagnostics, supplying animation, motion-aware styling, terminal media behavior, and ephemeral progress output without forcing higher-level code to manage timing or accessibility details itself.

At the center, tui/src/motion.rs is the façade other UI code calls when it wants an activity indicator or shimmered text. It routes those requests to the appropriate helpers while enforcing reduced-motion fallbacks so the interface remains usable in less dynamic modes. tui/src/shimmer.rs implements the actual shimmer effect, producing animated text styling in both full true-color terminals and simpler intensity-based fallbacks. tui/src/ascii_animation.rs provides the reusable time-based frame driver for animated ASCII widgets, while tui/src/frames.rs supplies the embedded frame sets, default cadence, and registry of available animations those widgets can choose from.

Outside the full-screen TUI, cli/src/doctor/progress.rs applies the same transient-output philosophy to command-line diagnostics, choosing between a temporary stderr status line and a silent reporter based on terminal capability and output mode. Together these pieces give the system consistent, capability-sensitive motion and progress feedback.
